summary.intent: Fix the font size in the reasoning dropdown items to visually match the model dropdown, then commit and push the changes.
summary.outcome: Identified that reasoning dropdown items lacked explicit font-size (inheriting body default instead of 0.78rem), applied the fix in reasoning-toggle.js, linted, and committed.

repo: Model dropdown items use a `.model-item-label` wrapper with explicit font-size: 0.78rem, but reasoning dropdown items set textContent directly on `.model-dropdown-item` without that wrapper, causing them to inherit the larger body font size.
`.model-combo-btn` sets font-size: 0.78rem at line 1098 of the stylesheet, shared by both model and reasoning combo buttons.
The model combo button applies max-width: 220px to constrain label text; the reasoning label had no such constraint.

code.finding: Reasoning dropdown items were rendered without a `.model-item-label` wrapper, so they inherited body font size rather than the intended 0.78rem. Fix applied directly in this file.

workflow: Linting was run on the modified JS file before committing, as a standard pre-commit step.

friction: The root cause required multiple grep passes (model-combo-btn, then model-dropdown-item) to trace the font-size inheritance chain before the discrepancy was identified.
